mechanism_narrative: A downward shift in FEDFUNDS signals easing financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month when inflation is not accelerating. The effect is strongest after recent rate cuts because investors reprice terminal policy expectations faster than earnings.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls at least 0.25pp over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered rate-cut episodes do not produce higher-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign turns negative when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent range signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower expected earnings, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is most reliable when payroll growth is also slowing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3pp above its 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-spike triggers do not coincide with below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS growth is not weakening.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. This works as a momentum-of-fundamentals signal rather than a valuation signal.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not outperform unconditional 5d SPX returns, or if the sign is persistently negative in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial activity and better cyclical earnings prospects, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect is strongest when output growth is broad rather than a one-month noise spike.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive-month industrial production gains fail to predict positive 60d SPX returns, or if the signal loses sign consistency in expanding regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound in HOUST signals improving interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, which supports equity returns over the next quarter through higher growth expectations. The signal requires an actual turn up from depressed levels, not merely a high absolute reading.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month average after being below it for 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing turn-up episodes do not lead to higher 60d SPX returns, especially when FEDFUNDS remains elevated and the rebound is not accompanied by easier policy.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level restrains equity multiples by keeping discount rates and financing costs elevated, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is still above its recent trend, because real rate pressure then remains restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if high-policy-rate episodes do not produce below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign is not more negative when CPIAUCSL inflation remains sticky.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDPC1 growth captures aggregate demand momentum; stronger-than-recent GDP growth raises forward earnings expectations and supports S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when GDP is revised sharply, because late revisions can remove the apparent growth impulse.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth exceeds its prior-quarter growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP acceleration does not forecast positive 20d SPX returns after publication, or if the effect vanishes once revisions materially change the growth sign.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signals broad labor-market strengthening, which lifts consumer income expectations and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is a joint condition: payroll gains without a falling unemployment rate are weaker evidence.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE falls within the same monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll-up and unemployment-down releases do not outperform baseline 5d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect without the joint condition.
